author: Scott Kosman
description: Run Claude Code inside Obsidian on macOS, Linux, and Windows: interactive terminal, quick-ask modal, and a built-in MCP server for structured vault access. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Scott Kosman
manifest.description: Run Claude Code inside your vault with an interactive terminal, quick-ask modal, and a built-in MCP server for structured vault access.
manifest.name: Glass

name: Glass

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description: (maintenance release — no user-facing changes)

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
The only change between 1.9.1 and 1.9.2 is the version bump itself — no other commits exist in that range.

(maintenance release — no user-facing changes)

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new

- Updated the Quick Ask model list to include Sonnet 5 and Fable 5

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new

- Terminal appearance: `letterSpacing` and `lineHeight` are now configurable settings.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new

- Fixed clipboard copy failing in the terminal and quick-ask views (issue #1)

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
All commits since 1.8.6 are version bumps, ESLint/lint cleanup, README updates, and a source revert to the stable 1.8.2 codebase — no functional changes.

(maintenance release — no user-facing changes)

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new

### Terminal input

- **Fixed macOS Option key characters not appearing in the terminal.** Option-key combinations (e.g. `⌥+2` for ™, `⌥+shift+2` for €, `⌥+4` for ¢) are now correctly captured and sent to the terminal, so composed special characters work as expected without interfering with normal shortcuts like Ctrl+C.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description: - **Plugin renamed to Glass** — Shorter, cleaner name. The plugin ID remains `blackglass` so your existing installation updates seamlessly.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new

### Performance improvements
- Fixed resize thrashing that occurred when dragging the sidebar or resizing the terminal pane — resizing is now smooth and responsive
- Fixed input handler memory leak that occurred when closing sessions, improving long-term stability and reducing memory accumulation

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new

### Clickable wikilinks in terminal output

Wikilinks (`[[Note Name]]`) in terminal output are now clickable and open directly in Obsidian, just like `.md` file paths. Previously only bare `.md` paths were recognized as links.

### Fixes

- Fixed an issue in the release script where preamble stripping could mangle release notes.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's New in v1.7.0

### Note picker

A new **@** button in the toolbar opens a fuzzy search picker over your vault. Selecting a note writes `@<path>` directly into the terminal, making it easy to reference vault files in Claude conversations without typing paths by hand. The same action is also available as an Obsidian command — **"Insert note reference into terminal"** — so you can bind it to a hotkey.

### Clickable vault links

Markdown file paths that appear in terminal output (e.g. from Claude referencing a note) are now **clickable**. Clicking a `.md` path opens that file in Obsidian immediately.

### Settings button in toolbar

A **⚙** button has been added to the toolbar as a direct shortcut to Blackglass settings — no need to dig through the Obsidian settings menu.

### Remote access

Blackglass now supports remote access to your vault via Claude Code's `/remote-control` command. See the README for setup details.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
This release rolls 1.6.3 and 1.6.4 together and is purely internal quality/code-health fixes. 

---

## What's new

### Code quality & Obsidian review improvements

This release focuses on internal code quality improvements that strengthen the plugin's standing with Obsidian's review process.

**TypeScript hygiene (1.6.3)**
- Replaced dynamic `require()` calls with static imports for better compatibility
- Switched to Obsidian's `FileSystemAdapter` API instead of unsafe type casts
- Added proper types throughout (`FontWeight`, `FontData`, `GitHubRelease`) and fixed floating promises — reducing the risk of unhandled async errors in edge cases

**ESLint integration (1.6.4)**
- Added `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` to enforce Obsidian's brand and acronym rules (e.g. correct casing for "Claude Code" and "MCP")
- Fixed all remaining lint warnings: unsafe `JSON.parse` assignments, unhandled promise returns in `revealLeaf` and `stop()`, and sentence-case issues in settings descriptions
- Plugin now ships with zero ESLint warnings; run `npm run lint` locally to verify

These changes are invisible to end users but improve reliability and ensure the plugin meets Obsidian's quality bar for community plugin distribution.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.6.2

**Bug fixes**

- Fixed the plugin settings panel heading to comply with Obsidian's UI guidelines. The word "Settings" has been removed from the heading, matching the convention Obsidian enforces across all plugins.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's New in Blackglass 1.6.1

### Obsidian Community Plugin Compatibility

This release resolves all issues flagged during Obsidian's plugin review process, making Blackglass eligible for the community plugins directory.

- **Settings headings** now follow Obsidian's UI guidelines for a consistent look in the Settings panel
- **Styles** are properly scoped as static CSS, preventing potential conflicts with other plugins
- **Lint directives** updated to meet Obsidian's code quality requirements
- **Minimum app version** corrected to accurately reflect Obsidian compatibility
- **Build artifacts** include attestations for supply chain verification

No functionality changes — this is a compliance and polish release.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.6.0

### Streaming Quick Ask

Quick Ask now streams Claude's response in real time instead of waiting for the full reply before displaying anything.

- **Typewriter animation** — text appears character by character as Claude generates it, so you can start reading immediately
- **Stop button** — cancel a response mid-stream at any time; whatever has arrived so far is rendered as the final answer
- Closing the modal cleanly cancels any in-flight request

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in Blackglass 1.5.3

### Model updates

- **Claude Opus 4.8 is now available** in the model selector. Opus 4.8 is Anthropic's latest and most capable model — select it from the model dropdown in plugin settings or the Quick Ask panel for your most demanding tasks.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in Blackglass 1.5.2

**Claude Opus 4.8 in Quick Ask**

Claude Opus 4.8 is now available as a model option in the Quick Ask selector. You can choose it when you want maximum capability for complex reasoning or nuanced questions, without leaving Obsidian.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in Blackglass 1.5.1

### Toolbar improvements

- **Wordmark** — the Blackglass name now appears in the toolbar for a cleaner, more polished look
- **Version label** — the current plugin version is displayed in the toolbar; pre-release builds are highlighted in amber so you always know what you're running
- **Labeled status indicators** — session and MCP status indicators now include text labels, making their meaning immediately clear at a glance

### MCP controls

- **Live MCP toggle** — enable or disable the vault MCP server from Settings without restarting the plugin; takes effect immediately
- **MCP status indicator** — see at a glance in the toolbar whether the vault MCP server is active

### Update checker

- Blackglass now checks for new versions and notifies you when an update is available, so you don't miss improvements

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in Blackglass 1.5.0

### Windows support is here! 

Blackglass now works on Windows. The terminal backend uses [pywinpty](https://github.com/spyder-ide/pywinpty) to provide a native PTY layer on Windows, bringing the same interactive Claude Code terminal experience to Windows users that macOS and Linux users already have.

To use Blackglass on Windows, ensure Python is installed and available on your PATH. The plugin will handle the rest.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's New in v1.4.0

### Live font customization

Font settings now apply instantly to the running terminal — no need to close and reopen the Blackglass panel to see changes take effect.

**Font weight picker** — a new setting shows the actual named weight variants available for your chosen font family (e.g. Light, SemiBold, ExtraBold), pulled from your system's installed fonts via the browser font enumeration API. For fonts that aren't enumerated, standard weight options are shown as a fallback.

Together, font family, size, and weight can all be tuned and previewed live, making it easier to dial in a terminal appearance that matches your vault theme or personal preference.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's New

### Bug Fixes

**Fixed powerline and status line rendering** — Characters used by powerline prompts and terminal status lines (such as branch indicators, segment separators, and other glyphs) now render correctly instead of appearing as missing or garbled symbols. This fix enables the `customGlyphs` option in the terminal, which is required for these characters to display as intended.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's New

### Terminal improvements

- **Clear terminal button** — Clear the terminal output at any time from the toolbar without restarting your session.
- **Session status indicator** — The toolbar now shows whether your Claude Code session is active, so you always know what's running at a glance.
- **Configurable scrollback** — Set how many lines of terminal history to retain in settings, useful if you work with long outputs or want to conserve memory.

### Theme compatibility

- **Third-party theme support** — The terminal now derives its colors from Obsidian's CSS variables instead of hardcoded values, so it adapts automatically to whatever theme you're using.

### Quick Ask

- **Loading spinner** — The Quick Ask modal now shows an animated spinner while waiting for a response, so it's clear when a request is in flight.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.2.1

### Bug fixes

- **Clickable links now open correctly in the terminal.** URLs displayed in Claude's output can be opened in your browser as expected — a regression in 1.2.0 caused them to silently fail.

### Documentation

- Clarified that Windows Quick Ask users are billed against their Agent SDK credits.
- Cleaned up duplicate entries in the Windows troubleshooting guide.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.2.0

### Windows support removed

Windows terminal support has been reverted. The ConPTY API — the modern Windows pseudo-terminal used to render the terminal — has a sizing bug that makes the terminal unusable: output wraps incorrectly and the layout breaks in ways that can't be worked around at the plugin level.

If you're on Windows, Blackglass is not supported in this release. Mac and Linux users are unaffected.

Windows support remains a goal. This will be revisited if a viable path around the ConPTY sizing issue is found.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.1.4

### Windows fix

- Fixed a regression on Windows where Claude Code sessions failed to start. The previous release introduced a `cmd.exe` wrapper that broke session initialization; this release reverts to launching `conhost` directly, restoring reliable terminal sessions on Windows.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in v1.1.3

### Windows improvements

- Fixed a terminal sizing issue on Windows where the Claude Code session could launch with incorrect dimensions. The terminal now sets the correct column and row count via `mode con` before starting Claude, preventing layout glitches and truncated output on ConPTY-based sessions.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in v1.1.2

### Windows fix

- Fixed a startup failure on some Windows systems where the terminal session could not launch because `conhost.exe` was not found on the system PATH. Blackglass now uses the absolute path to `conhost.exe`, so ConPTY sessions start reliably regardless of how your environment is configured.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in Blackglass 1.1.1

### Windows fixes

- **Improved terminal stability on Windows** — Claude Code now spawns directly instead of routing through `conhost.exe`, reducing launch overhead and eliminating a class of startup failures.
- **Fixed unhandled PTY errors on Windows** — a missing error handler could cause the plugin to crash silently when the terminal process exited unexpectedly; errors are now caught and surfaced cleanly.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's New in Blackglass 1.1.0

### Windows Support

Blackglass now runs on Windows. The plugin uses `conhost.exe --headless` to provide a compatible terminal environment, so Windows users can embed Claude Code in Obsidian with the same vault-aware experience previously available only on macOS and Linux.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in Blackglass 1.0.7

### Maintenance

- **Version badge stays current automatically** — the badge displayed on the Blackglass website now updates automatically with each release, so the version shown there always reflects the latest release without manual intervention.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's New

### Windows Support

If Blackglass can't find the Claude binary on Windows, it now shows a targeted hint explaining how to fix your PATH. This addresses a common setup issue where Claude Code is installed but not discoverable because the Windows `PATH` environment variable isn't configured to include it.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in Blackglass 1.0.5

### Now available in the Obsidian Community Plugin Directory

Blackglass can now be installed directly from Obsidian's built-in plugin browser — no more manual installation required. Search for "Blackglass" in **Settings → Community plugins → Browse** to install or update.

### Fixes

- Removed unused CSS rules for terminal text decoration (underline/strikethrough) that had no effect with the canvas renderer, reducing minor style noise.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's new in Blackglass 1.0.4

### Under the hood

- **Reduced plugin footprint** — removed an unnecessary dependency (`builtin-modules`), trimming the plugin's size and reducing install surface.
- **Improved style compatibility** — switched to longhand CSS `text-decoration` properties, which resolves potential rendering inconsistencies across Obsidian themes and versions.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's New

### Installation

- The plugin is now distributed as individual files (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) instead of a zip archive, in line with Obsidian's plugin requirements. If you're installing manually, download each file separately and place them in your `.obsidian/plugins/blackglass/` folder.

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
## What's New in 1.0.2

This is a maintenance release with no functional changes. It resolves compatibility issues with the Obsidian community plugin marketplace.

### Marketplace compatibility fixes

- Fixed release tag format so the plugin installs and updates correctly through the Obsidian community plugin browser
- Updated plugin description to meet Obsidian marketplace naming requirements

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description: **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/humantorch/blackglass/compare/v1.0.0...1.0.1

releases.author: humantorch
releases.description:
Initial release. Interactive Claude Code terminal embedded in Obsidian with vault-aware context injection.

**Installation:** Download `blackglass-1.0.0.zip`, extract the three files (`main.js`, `styles.css`, `manifest.json`) into `.obsidian/plugins/blackglass/` in your vault, then enable the plugin in Settings → Community Plugins.

**Requirements:** macOS or Linux, Python 3, Claude Code CLI on PATH.
releases.name: Blackglass v1.0.0
